text:
Once upon a time in the verdant heart of the Enchanted Forest, there lived a gentle giant named Thorn. Thorn was no ordinary tree but a majestic fir, tall and strong with branches that whispered secrets of the past. One sunny morning, as the golden rays danced through his leaves, Thorn noticed a tiny bird, injured and barely alive, perched on one of his lower branches.

Thorn, ever kind, extended his boughs to offer shelter and comfort to the little bird. The bird, whose name was Seren, had flown too close to the forest edge and collided with a branch. She needed rest and food to regain her strength. Thorn knew just what to do.

From beneath his roots, Thorn summoned a stream of sweet, fragrant sap that he slowly dripped onto Seren's beak. The sap nourished her, and her feathers began to shimmer once more. Over the days, Thorn taught Seren about the forest and how to navigate its many wonders
